macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Fed Expected to Hold Rates Steady at 3.50%-3.75% in Upcoming Meeting
items.summary: The Federal Reserve is widely anticipated to keep interest rates unchanged at 3.50%-3.75% for a second straight meeting on April 29, amid rising oil prices from the war with Iran and persistent inflation above 2%. This decision precedes Jerome Powell's final press conference before his term ends on May 15, 2026.

items.title: Oil Price Spike Complicates Fed's Inflation Fight Ahead of Powell's Exit
items.summary: Brent crude has surged around 50% since the war with Iran began, pushing March CPI to its largest increase in nearly four years and raising questions on whether it's temporary or sustained inflation pressure. Economists still expect two rate cuts this year, potentially delayed.

items.title: Key US Economic Data Releases Scheduled This Week Including PCE and Jobs Reports
items.summary: Upcoming events include Consumer Confidence on April 28, Initial Jobless Claims and PCE inflation on April 30, and ISM Manufacturing PMI on May 1, with recent jobless claims at 214K expected to dip slightly.

items.title: Major Tech Earnings from Microsoft, Amazon, Meta, and Google Set for April 29
items.summary: Earnings reports from leading tech giants including Microsoft, Amazon, Meta, and Google are due on April 29, coinciding with the Fed decision and potentially influencing broader market sentiment.

items.title: Justice Department Ends Criminal Probe into Fed Chair Powell
items.summary: The US Justice Department has dropped its criminal investigation into Jerome Powell related to the Federal Reserve's building renovations, which Powell claimed was politically motivated.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Crashes Below $77,000 in Sudden Sell-Off, Triggering Over $300 Million in Liquidations
items.summary: Bitcoin dropped from a high of $79,480 to $76,960, losing the critical $77,000 support amid high-volume selling and nearly $300 million in crypto futures liquidations, mostly long positions. The Fear and Greed Index plunged from 62 (Greed) to 38 (Fear) as oil price surges weighed on market sentiment.

items.title: Scallop DeFi Protocol on Sui Network Hacked for $140,000 in SUI Tokens
items.summary: A Sui-based DeFi protocol named Scallop suffered a hack early today, with attackers stealing approximately 150,000 SUI tokens worth over $140,000. This incident highlights ongoing security risks in the DeFi sector amid broader market volatility.

items.title: Bitcoin Faces Major Volatility This Week Ahead of Fed Meeting, GDP, and PCE Data
items.summary: Bitcoin is poised for heightened volatility over the next 48 hours due to the Federal Reserve's April 29 FOMC decision, followed by Q1 GDP and PCE inflation data releases. The crowded $80,000 resistance zone contributed to today's flash crash below $78,000.

items.title: XRP Futures Open Interest Surges 2.5% Amid Bitcoin's Reversal from $79,500
items.summary: Open interest in XRP futures rose nearly 2.5% in the past 24 hours, the largest increase among major tokens, even as Bitcoin reversed from $79,500 to $77,800 due to oil price pressures and failed $80,000 breakout.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Google Introduces Hope Architecture to End Frozen LLMs and Enable Continual Learning
items.summary: Google's new 'Nested Learning' paper proposes the Hope architecture, mimicking human brain memory to allow LLMs to learn continuously without catastrophic forgetting, potentially rivaling Transformers. The research claims strong performance gains over baselines in continual learning tasks.
items.source: YouTube - The End of Frozen LLMs? (Google's Hope Explained)

items.title: Korean Researchers Develop Preheating Method to Reduce AI Hallucinations
items.summary: A Korean research team created a 'preheating' stage inspired by human brain development, feeding meaningless data to new AIs to teach them 'I know nothing' before real training, reducing false information. The method suits new conversational and autonomous driving AIs and was published in Nature Machine Intelligence.
items.source: YouTube - 'MacBook' in the Annals of the Joseon Dynasty?.. How to ...

items.title: Palantir's Manifesto on AI Warfare Sparks Global Outrage
items.summary: Palantir published a 22-point manifesto envisioning autonomous weapons and deep tech-government integration, intensifying debates on Silicon Valley's military ties amid accusations of use in conflicts like Gaza.
items.source: YouTube - Varoufakis on Palantir, AI warfare, and the rise of tech lordism

items.title: China Blocks Meta's Acquisition of AI Startup Manis
items.summary: China has blocked Meta's attempt to acquire AI startup Manis, amid ongoing tensions in AI investments and technology deals.
items.source: YouTube - Markets DON'T CARE About War... EARNINGS SEASON IS HERE

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Microsoft and OpenAI Amend Partnership Deal
items.summary: Microsoft and OpenAI have updated their agreement, allowing OpenAI to offer products across any cloud provider without revenue sharing to Microsoft. Microsoft retains model usage rights until 2032 and removes prior IP clauses tied to AGI.

items.title: Meta Signs Deal for 1GW Space Solar Energy
items.summary: Meta has agreed to purchase up to 1GW of solar energy from space via Overview Energy's satellite technology, with a demonstration planned for 2028 to power AI data centers.

items.title: Meta Plans Layoffs of 8000 Employees Amid Restructuring
items.summary: Meta is undergoing major internal restructuring, laying off 8000 employees and not filling 6000 open roles as part of heavy investments in other areas.

items.title: US Senator Warren Raises Concerns Over Nvidia's Slurm Acquisition
items.summary: Senator Elizabeth Warren expressed worries about Nvidia's purchase of SchedMD, which controls Slurm software used in US government supercomputers, citing national security and competition risks.

items.title: X Launches Dedicated Messaging App XChat for iOS
items.summary: X (formerly Twitter) has released XChat, a standalone messaging app for iOS users.

items.title: Instagram Launches Standalone Instants App
items.summary: Instagram has quietly introduced Instants, a new app focused on specific features separate from the main platform.

items.title: Apple Fixes iOS Notification Bug Exposing Deleted Messages
items.summary: Apple has patched a vulnerability in iOS that allowed deleted messages to be exposed through notifications.
